coredata.dc:title: A sandwiched photoelectrochemical biosensing platform for detecting Cytokeratin-19 fragments based on Ag2S-sensitized BiOI/Bi2S3 heterostructure amplified by sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Shitao
Wang, Chao
Wu, Tingting
Fan, Dawei
Hu, Lihua
Wang, Huan
Wei, Qin
Wu, Dan
coredata.dc:description: A sandwiched photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunosensor based on BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S was designed for the quantitative detection of cytokeratin-19 fragments (CYFRA21-1) in serum. In this work, due to the intervention of the narrow band gap Bi2S3, the absorption of the light source by the BiOI/Bi2S3 heterostructure has been significantly enhanced. Meanwhile, the matched band structure of BiOI, Bi2S3 and Ag2S promoted the rapid transfer of electrons between the conduction bands and effectively inhibited the recombination of electron-hole pairs, thus enhanced the photoelectric signals. Sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots (S,N-CQDs) with up-conversion luminescence properties provided more light energy for the base materials. On the other hand, S,N-CQDs were combined with Ab2 through polydopamine (PDA), as secondary antibody labels, further enhanced the sensitivity of the sensor. Herein, the linear range of the sensor was from 0.001 to 100 ng mL−1 and the detection limit was 1.72 pg mL−1. In addition, the sensor provides a feasible way for the detection of tumor markers due to its excellent selectivity, repeatability and good stability.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Bismuth complex
Sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots
Cytokeratin-19 fragments
Photoelectrochemical immunoassay

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Construction of signal-amplified sandwich PEC immunosensor
Characterization of the prepared PEC immunosensor

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: We gratefully acknowledge the financial support of the Young Taishan Scholars Program of Shandong Province ( tsqn201909124 ), the National Natural Science Foundation of China ( 21775054 ), the Project of “20 items of University” of Jinan ( 2019GXRC018 ) and the National Key Scientific Instrument and Equipment Development Project of China ( 21627809 ). All the authors express their sincere thanks.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM image of BiOI (a), BiOI/Bi2S3 (b) and BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S (c); TEM image of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S (d); XRD patterns (e) and the EDS spectrum of the BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S electrode(f).
XPS spectra of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composites (A). High-resolution XPS spectra of I 3d (B); O 1s (C); Bi 4f, S 2p (D) and Ag 3d (E).

Photocurrent responses of BiOI, BiOI/Bi2S3 and BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S (A); Stability of the BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S electrode with 20 on/off irradiation cycles (B) EIS (C) and Photocurrent responses (D): (a) ITO; (b) ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S; (c) ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S/Ab1; (d) ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S/Ab1/BSA; (e) ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S/Ab1/BSA/CYFRA21–1; (f) ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S/Ab1/BSA/CYFRA21–1/PDA@S,N-CQDs@Ab2

(A) Photocurrent response and (B) logarithmic calibration curve of the designed PEC immunosensor with different concentration of CYFRA21-1 (a–l: 0.001 ng mL−1 - 100 ng mL−1); (C) Specificity detection of the prepared PEC sensor for CYFRA 21-1: (a) Blank, (b) Blank + 10 ng mL−1 PSA, (c) Blank + 10 ng mL−1 CEA, (d) Blank + 10 ng mL−1 glucose, (e) 0.1 ng mL−1 CYFRA 21-1, (f) 10 ng mL−1 PSA + (e), (g) 10 ng mL−1 CEA + (e), (h) 10 ng mL−1 glucose + (e); (D) Photocurrent responses under several on/off irradiation cycles for 400 s (c CYFRA21-1 = 0.1 ng mL−1). Error bars = SD (n = 5).
Schematic diagram of sensor construction process.
Possible electron-transfer mechanism of the PEC sensor.

head.ce:title: A sandwiched photoelectrochemical biosensing platform for detecting Cytokeratin-19 fragments based on Ag2S-sensitized BiOI/Bi2S3 heterostructure amplified by sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, University of Jinan, Jinan, 250022, Shandong, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong
School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, University of Jinan, Jinan, 250022, Shandong, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Rehabilitation, Shandong Provincial Hospital Affiliated to Shandong First Medical University, Jinan, 250021, Shandong, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Shandong Provincial Hospital Affiliated to Shandong First Medical University

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Rehabilitation, Shandong Provincial Hospital Affiliated to Shandong First Medical University, Jinan, 250021, Shandong, China

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: These authors (#) contributed equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A sandwiched photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunosensor based on BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S was designed for the quantitative detection of cytokeratin-19 fragments (CYFRA21-1) in serum. In this work, due to the intervention of the narrow band gap Bi2S3, the absorption of the light source by the BiOI/Bi2S3 heterostructure has been significantly enhanced. Meanwhile, the matched band structure of BiOI, Bi2S3 and Ag2S promoted the rapid transfer of electrons between the conduction bands and effectively inhibited the recombination of electron-hole pairs, thus enhanced the photoelectric signals. Sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots (S,N-CQDs) with up-conversion luminescence properties provided more light energy for the base materials. On the other hand, S,N-CQDs were combined with Ab2 through polydopamine (PDA), as secondary antibody labels, further enhanced the sensitivity of the sensor. Herein, the linear range of the sensor was from 0.001 to 100 ng mL−1 and the detection limit was 1.72 pg mL−1. In addition, the sensor provides a feasible way for the detection of tumor markers due to its excellent selectivity, repeatability and good stability.

ce:keyword.ce:text: Sulfur and nitrogen co-doped carbon quantum dots

ce:section.ce:para: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunoassay sensor has played a more important part in many fields in recent decades because its advantages of uncomplicated device, high sensitivity and low background signals (Li et al., 2017b; Mei et al., 2018; Siavash Moakhar et al., 2015). With the maturity of technology, PEC detection methods are also able to show its talents in fields of biological samples (Li et al. 2014, 2017a; Zhao et al. 2016a, 2016b), such as DNA detection (Li et al., 2017c), protein detection (Tang et al., 2008) and cell detection (Li et al., 2019). Photoactive materials, as the core of PEC sensors, can significantly affect the performance of PEC sensors. Therefore, it has attracted a lot of attention from numerous researchers (Zhang et al., 2018).

In recent years, many nanostructured semiconductors, such as TiO2 (Zhang et al., 2019), g-C3N4 (Xue et al., 2015), Bi2WO6 (Li et al., 2018), MoS2 (Ye et al., 2016) and ZnS (Zhang et al., 2020) have been applied to the preparation of PEC sensor. However, the low energy conversion efficiency and rapid charge recombination defects of these materials are quickly revealed (Jiang et al., 2017). Therefore, photoelectric materials with stable activity and fast electron hole separation are still worth exploring and expecting.

To date, various compounds of Bi metal have been used in photoactive materials to prepare sensors with excellent performance (Han et al., 2018a). Among the numerous Bi metal compounds, BiOX(X=F, Cl, Br, I), an ideal photoelectrically active materials, was widely used in the construction of PEC sensors due to its suitable band gaps, chemically stability, nontoxicity, and corrosion resistance (Chen et al., 2016; Di et al., 2015; Jahurul Islam et al., 2016; Zhao et al., 2017). Especially, BiOI exhibited superior visible-light-harvesting ability due to its narrow band gap (1.7–1.9 eV) (Song et al., 2018). However, the application of BiOI was limited by the low photoelectric conversion efficiency, an important constraint was the separation of photogenerated electron/hole pairs. Actually, not all electron pairs can be effectively separated, most of them would recombine, and only few would be completely separated and consumed by the redox reaction (Zhang et al., 2016). In order to overcome this defects, the strategy of combining BiOI with other semiconductor materials to form heterojunction was proposed and applied (Han et al., 2018b; Sun et al., 2017). Bi2S3 was widely adopted to construct composite thanks to its low band gap(∼1.2 eV), such as Bi2S3/UiO-66 (Chen et al., 2020), MoS2/Bi2S3 (Jiang et al., 2020). However, BiOI/Bi2S3 heterojunction were used in the research of photoelectric sensors have rarely been reported.

CYFRA21-1 has important research value in cancer detection. It is mainly derived from tumor diseases such as esophageal cancer and lung cancer, it can be released into the blood after tumor cell lysis and necrosis. The content of the substance in lung cancer patients is significantly higher than that in normal people. Therefore, CYFRA21-1 was used to diagnose lung diseases and differentiate between benign and malignant lung diseases, which has a higher diagnostic value (Kammer et al., 2019; Okamura et al., 2013; Zhao et al., 2020). So far, multiple methods have been applied in the detection of CYFRA21-1, for instance, electrochemical detection (Hu et al., 2020), fluorescence quantitative PCR (Li et al., 2020), magnetic immunoassay (Cao et al., 2020) and enzyme-linked reaction adsorption (Falzarano et al., 2013). However, the development of a sensitively responsive, simple structure, and usableness sensor for detecting CYFRA21-1 is still an urgent problem to be overcome.

In the current work, a sandwiched PEC immunosensor based on BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S for CYFRA21-1 detection was successfully constructed. After BiOI/Bi2S3 heterojunction successfully anchored to indium-tin oxide (ITO) electrode, Ag2S was modified on the surface of the heterojunction to enhance the photoelectric response of the materials to visible light. The secondary antibody label composed of S,N-CQDs and PDA further improved the sensitivity of the sensor. CYFRA21-1 was chosen as the target to demonstrate the practicality of the sensor. The sensor was proved to provide a novel and practical method for real-time detection of tumor markers.
Bi(NO3)3·5H2O was obtained from Tianjin ZhiYuan Reagent Co., Ltd. KI was obtained from Shanghai Haohong Biomedical Technology Co., Ltd. Other information was shown in Supplementary material.

BiOI/Bi2S3 composite was synthesized on the basis of the reported literature (Guo et al., 2017). 5 mmol KI and 1 mmol Bi(NO3)3·5H2O were dissolved in the mixed solution of 5 mL ethanol and 3 mL deionized water, and ultrasound to dark red. The solution was centrifuged and the supernatant was retained. 5 mL of the above supernatant was added dropwise to 100 mL 1% tween-20 solution under vigorous stirring. Then, the clear solution was filtered several times by 0.22 μm filter unit and the clear orange filtrate was heated to 180 °C for 320 min in a high temperature container. After naturally cooling, the obtained samples were washed several times with deionized water and ethanol, and then dried in vacuum. Finally, BiOI was ground into powder for use. The powder of BiOI was shown in Fig. S6A.

20 mg of prepared BiOI powder was added to 40 mL deionized water, which was evenly dispersed by ultrasound for 15min. Then, 4 mL 5 × 10−3 mol L−1 TAA solution was slowly added dropwise to introduce sulfur. After continuing ultrasound for a while, the above mixed solution was transferred to a Teflon-lined stainless-steel autoclave and heated to 180 °C for 30 min. The as-prepared products were washed with deionized water and anhydrous ethanol three times respectively when cooling to room temperature. Finally, BiOI/Bi2S3 was freeze-dried for collection. The powder of BiOI/Bi2S3 was shown in Fig. S6B.

PDA NS was synthesized by oxidative polymerization. First, 100 mg of dopamine was dissolved in 100 mL 0.01 mol L−1 tris-HCl solution (pH=8.8), followed by 50 mL isopropyl alcohol. After the mixed solution was stirred in the dark for a day, the resulting products were rinsed three times with deionized water and freeze-dried. Finally, the sample was dissolved in 4 mL deionized water and stored in a refrigerator at 4 °C.

According to previous reports, S,N co-doped CQDs were synthesized via one-step hydrothermal protocol (Liao et al., 2018). the solution of 0.64 g citric acid and 1.01 g thiamine hydrochloride was dispersed into 25 mL deionized water, and the obtained solution was sonicated until becoming clear liquid. Then, 2 mol L−1 NaOH solution was used to adjust pH to neutral, after that, the mixed solution was heated 160 °C for 5 h. And then, the prepared S,N-CQDs were centrifuged (9000 r/min for 15 min) when cooling to room temperature, and the supernatant was taken and dialyzed with deionized water for 24 h. After the completion of dialysis, it was placed in a refrigerator at 4 °C and kept away from light.

500 μL PDA was diluted to 1.5 mL deionized water (solution a). 50 μL EDC/NHS was added to 500 μL S,N-CQDs, shaking at 4 °C for 2 h (solution b). Then, solution a and b were mixed and continue to shake for 6 h (solution c). After 1 mL Ab2 (2 μg mL−1) was dissolved in solution c and shaken for 6 h, the final solution was centrifuged and dispersed in 1 mL PBS for storage.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Construction of signal-amplified sandwich PEC immunosensor
ce:section.ce:para: Before the construction work starts, ITO (0.6*2.0 cm2) was washed thoroughly with acetone, ethanol and deionized water, and then dried under nitrogen.

Scheme 1 showed the preparation process of PEC immunosensor. The solution of 10 μL 4 mg mL−1 BiOI/Bi2S3 nanocomposite was modified on ITO, incubated for 1 h at room temperature, and then soaked in 0.1 mol L−1 Na2S solution for 30 s. The electrode was rinsed with deionized water and then moved into 0.1 mol L−1 AgNO3 solution for 30 s. So far, ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S has been successfully prepared. Subsequently, 3 μL 3 mol L−1 TGA was added. Then, 2 μL EDC/NHS was modified after incubation for another 30 min. 5 μL 1 μg mL−1 Ab1 was dropped on the surface of the electrode, the electrode was washed with deionized water. After drying, BSA (3 μL 0.1 wt%) was embellished on the surface of the electrode to seal off the non-specific binding sites. Then, 5 μL CYFRA21-1 with different concentrations (0.001–100 ng mL−1) were modified and incubated for 30 min. After the immunoreactions between Ab1 and CYFRA21-1, 5 μL PDA@S,N-CQDs@Ab2 were deposited on the electrode surface. The preparation process of the PEC sensor was completed at room temperature and stored in a refrigerator at 4 °C. The electrode washed with PBS after each modification step.

The PEC sensor was used in electrochemical workstation for photocurrent detection (CHI 760E Chenhua Instrument Company, Shanghai, China). Three-electrode system was applied: prepared sensor as working electrode, Pt electrode acted as counter electrode and a saturated calomel electrode served as reference. The photocurrent measurement was performed at room temperature in 10 mL of PBS (pH = 7.4, 0.1 mol L−1), and ascorbic acid (AA) (0.1 mol L−1) as an electron donor. A LED lamp (100 W, white light) was utilized as excitation light and switched on and off every 10 s under −0.1 V potential. After the human serum was centrifuged for three times, the supernatant was taken as the initial sample and stored in a refrigerator at 4 °C for later use. Moreover, the study was approved by the local ethics committee of the Hospital and carried out in strict accordance with the Helsinki Declaration (2013). Informed consent has been obtained from the volunteers participating in the project.

As shown in Fig. 1 A and Fig. 1B, BiOI has layered chocolate-cookie structure, the BiOI/Bi2S3 synthesized on the basis of BiOI inherits the morphology of BiOI, and it can be clearly seen that the surface of the materials were covered with a thin film, the morphology after modification with Ag2S was shown in Fig. 1C, BiOI/Bi2S3 was completely covered by Ag2S. Fig. 1D clearly showed that the size of the materials was about 200 nm.

The XRD analysis of BiOI, BiOI/Bi2S3 and BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S were implemented. As depicted in Fig. 1E, the XRD patterns of BiOI and BiOI/Bi2S3 were basically the same as previously reported (Guo et al., 2017). All peaks of BiOI can correspond in the standard phase (JCPDS No. 10-0445), the synthesized composite materials can obviously correspond to the standard phase of Bi2S3 (JCPDS No. 17-0320) and Ag2S (JCPDS No. 14-0072), which provided a strong basis for the successful synthesis of the materials. The EDS test results were shown in Fig. 1F and Fig. S1. I, O, Bi, S and Ag elements were existed in BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composite materials, which further proved that the materials were successfully prepared.

Fig. 2 A displayed the XPS spectra of the as-prepared materials. The peaks at 627.5 eV and 616.0 eV in Fig. 2B correspond to I 3d3/2 and I 3d5/2 of I− (Wang et al., 2020); The O 1s peaks at 529.3 eV and 528.2 eV were provided by O in BiOI (Fig. 2C). The peaks position information of S and Bi were shown in Fig. 2D, the peaks with binding energy of 160.0 eV and 161.2 eV were classical Bi 4f7/2 and Bi 4f5/2, respectively, weak Bi peaks detected at 164.8 eV and 157.5 eV, which confirmed that the valence state of Bi in the heterojunction was +3 (Cao et al., 2012), the 2p peak of S appears near 161.6 ev. In addition, the interaction of S and Bi made the peak position slightly shifted. The peaks located at 364.9 eV and 370.9 eV in Fig. 2E correspond to Ag 3d5/2 and Ag 3d3/2 (Di et al., 2019). Therefore, the XPS results proved the coexistence of BiOI, Bi2S3 and Ag2S in BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composites.

UV–Vis DRS of BiOI, BiOI/Bi2S3 and BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composites were shown in Fig. S2A. The absorption edge of BiOI was about 650 nm, the absorption of BiOI/Bi2S3 and BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S was significantly stronger than that of BiOI. It can be clearly seen that heterojunctions sensitized by Ag2S has stronger absorption capacity, indicating that Ag2S quantum dots have achieved satisfactory results as a photosensitizer for BiOI/Bi2S3 heterojunctions. In addition, the band gap characteristics of the materials were shown in Fig. S2B and Fig. S2C, the calculated band gap energies of BiOI and Bi2S3 were calculated to be 1.77 eV and 1.45 eV respectively, which was basically consistent with the literature reports (Jahurul Islam et al., 2016). Fig. S2D showed the calculated gap energy of Ag2S was about 1.41 eV.

The flat-band potentials of BiOI, Bi2S3 and Ag2S were explored through the Mott-Schottky curve in order to further prove the electron transfer mechanism of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S, and the results were shown in Fig. S3. Since the flat band potential of an n-type semiconductor was similar to the conduction band, and the flat-band potentials (Vfb) of BiOI was −0.3V vs SCE, the conduction band of BiOI was calculated to be +0.34V vs RHE according to E (RHE) = E (SCE) + 0.0591 pH + 0.24. The conduction bands of Bi2S3 and Ag2S were +0.22 and + 0.02V vs RHE. Herein, the stepwise energy band structure provided more favorable conditions for the separation and transfer of electron hole pairs.

The electronic transfer path of the signal-amplified PEC sensor was explained in Scheme 2 . Clearly, the separation of electrons and holes of BiOI, Bi2S3 and Ag2S was not difficult under visible light irradiation, the matched band gap structure supported the rapid transfer of electrons between the CB of Ag2S, Bi2S3 and BiOI, and the electrons were transferred to the ITO to be detected. At the same time, the photo-generated holes were transferred to the surface of the materials in the opposite direction and were consumed in time by AA. On the other hand, S,N-CQDs with up-conversion luminescence properties convert near-infrared light into visible light (Huang et al., 2017; Liao et al., 2018; Tian et al., 2015), thereby providing more light energy for the BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composite materials.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of the prepared PEC immunosensor
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 3 A showed the photocurrent response of the materials. Compared with BiOI, BiOI/Bi2S3 heterojunction has significantly improved photocurrent intensity, this was due to the tight bonding of the band structure between BiOI and Bi2S3. In particular, the current intensity has increased by more than 4 times after Ag2S sensitization, which confirmed that Ag2S enhanced the absorption of light sources and effectively promoted electron transfer. As confirmed in Fig. 3B, no noticeable photocurrent change occurred in ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S after a continuous 400 s switching cycle, which suggesting the excellent stability of the electrode.

After the electrode was assembled layer by layer, the electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) was used to characterize the interface properties of the prepared electrode. As shown in Fig. 3C, the equivalent circuit including solution resistance (R s), electron transfer resistance (R et), Warburg impedance (Z ω) and double layer capacitance (C dl) was shown in the illustration. For EIS Nyquist parts, the semicircular arc was represented by the Ret, The linear part of the tail was corresponded to the diffusion limited process. The bare ITO electrode showed a curve with a small semicircular arc (curve a), the Ret value increased slightly due to the adhesion of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S (curve b). Subsequently, with the further modification of Ab1 (curve c), BSA (curve d), CYFRA21-1 (curve e) and PDA@S,N-CQDs@Ab2 (curve f), the Ret value showed a gradually increasing trend, which was caused by the large steric hindrance of the protein.

The photocurrent of the sensor during the construction process was shown in Fig. 3D, after BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S was bound to the electrode, the photocurrent intensity was the maximum (curve b), and then, the photocurrent gradually decreased after Ab1(curve c), BSA (curve d) and CYFRA21-1 (curve e) decorated on the ITO/BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S electrode. However, a significant increase was observed in photocurrent when PDA@S,N-CQDs@Ab2 were continued to be modified on the electrode, which enhancing effect could be due to the intrinsic nature of visible light absorption and up-conversion fluorescence of such S,N-CQDs. In short, the designed PEC immunosensor has been successfully prepared.

In order to obtain the excellent performance of the PEC immunosensor, some experimental conditions have been explored. Fig. S4A exhibited the relationship between photocurrent intensity and the concentration of BiOI/Bi2S3 heterojunction, as the concentration of BiOI/Bi2S3 changes from 1.0 mg mL−1 to 5.0 mg mL−1, the photocurrent reached its highest peak at 4 mg mL−1. The transfer of electrons to the electrode interface would be hindered when the content of BiOI/Bi2S3 was excessive. The amount of modified Ag2S on the BiOI/Bi2S3 electrode could be adjusted by the number of Ag2S coating layers. It could be seen from Fig. S4B, the intensity of photocurrent increased gradually with the increase of assembly times, and the maximum photocurrent intensity was obtained after 5 coatings. The photocurrent would be decreased when the load of Ag2S continues to increase, which was caused by the base materials falling off the electrode surface.

The pH value of the PBS will have a huge impact on sensor performance. As shown in Fig. S4C, the photocurrent of the sensor reached the maximum when the pH of PBS was 7.4, because the neutral environment was conducive to the maintenance of protein activity, and excessive acid or alkali would cause damage to the protein. In addition, the concentration of AA was used to further explore the performance of the sensor, Fig. S4D indicated that photocurrent reached a plateau at 0.1 mol L−1. Therefore, 0.1 mol L−1 AA was employed in the next experiment.

The constructed sensor was applied to measure the photoelectric response of different concentrations of CYFRA21-1, it can be seen from Fig. 4 A that the photocurrent increased with the increase of CYFRA21-1 (0.001–100 ng mL−1) concentration. In addition, the calibration equation was obtained by linearly fitting the logarithm of the antigen concentration and the photocurrent intensity (Fig. 4B), the obtained regression equation was I = 7.367 + 1.688 lg c (ng mL−1) (R2=0.996), with the limit of detection of 1.72 pg mL−1, compared with other detection methods, the constructed PEC sensor showed superior performance (Table S2).

Some potential substances in organisms such as PSA, CEA and glucose were used as interferences to measure the specificity of PEC sensor. As can be seen from Fig. 4C that the incubation of interfering substances did not cause a significant change in the photocurrent on the blank electrode in the absence of CYFRA21-1 (a, b, c, d). No obvious photocurrent fluctuations were also observed when incubating the above interference on the electrodes (e, f, g, h) in the presence of CYFRA21-1. This showed that the PEC immunosensor had excellent selectivity and specificity for CYFRA21-1. The constructed sensor was used as the working electrode for stability test, the results were shown in Fig. 4D. The photocurrent remained stable under the continuous 400s of cycling lights (the RSD = 0.23%). Fig. S5 showed the repeatability of the sensor, five electrodes with the same variable were prepared to detect CYFRA21-1. The RSD was calculated to be 1.7%, which provided evidence for the excellent reproducibility of the sensor.

In order to determine the applicability of the PEC immunosensor under actual conditions, the recovery rate of CYFRA21-1 at different concentrations was determined by the standard addition method, detailed data was presented in Table S3. It can be seen intuitively that the RSD range was 1.0%–1.8% with recovery from 98.4% to 101%. In addition, ELISA was used as a reference method to validate the proposed method in the detection of serum samples, and the test results were consistent with our method. Besides, the practicability of the sensor was further verified by detecting the content of CYFRA21-1 in the serum of healthy people and patients. The results in Table S4 indicated that the sensor has a broad application prospect in the detection of non-small cell lung cancer.

In general, a PEC immunosensor based on BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S composites was firstly prepared successfully and the matched band structure of the composites promoted the separation of electron-hole pairs. In addition, the up-conversion luminescence function of S,N-CQDs further improved the intensity of the light source and enhanced the sensitivity of the sensor. The analysis of actual samples showed that this sensor provided a feasible method for early diagnosis of disease. However, the efficiency of electron hole separation of photoactive materials is still an obstacle to the performance of the sensor. In the future, the exploration of photoactive materials with better performance is an important way to reduce the detection limit of sensors.

Shitao Zhang: Conceptualization, Data curation, writing original draft. Chao Wang: Data curation, Formal analysis. Tingting Wu: Methodology, Data curation, review & editing. Dawei Fan: Formal analysis. Lihua Hu: Formal analysis. Huan Wang: Formal analysis. Qin Wei: Funding acquisition, Formal analysis. Dan Wu: Funding acquisition, Formal analysis. Project administration.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing fnancial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We gratefully acknowledge the financial support of the Young Taishan Scholars Program of Shandong Province (tsqn201909124), the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21775054), the Project of “20 items of University” of Jinan (2019GXRC018) and the National Key Scientific Instrument and Equipment Development Project of China (21627809). All the authors express their sincere thanks.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
Reagents and Apparatus, EDS element map of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S heterojunction, UV-Vis DRS diffuse-reflectance spectra of BiOI/Bi2S3/Ag2S heterojunction, exploration of the optimum experimental conditions, specificity detection of CYFRA 21-1 and the reproducibility of the PEC immunosensor, analysis of real samples. The following is the Supplementary data to this article:
Multimedia component 1 Multimedia component 1

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bios.2021.113703.
